source.title: Demo reviewed pelvic pain early pregnancy case
source.citation: Local reviewed educational case file for Diagnostician demo content.
source.raw_text: De-identified educational case source assembled and reviewed for demo validation.

case.title: Pelvic pain with early pregnancy
case.chief_complaint: Lower abdominal pain and vaginal spotting.
teaching_points: Reproductive-age patients with pelvic pain and bleeding need pregnancy testing early.
A positive pregnancy test without an intrauterine gestation on ultrasound is a high-risk pattern.
Hemodynamic status determines urgency and management.

facts.label: Opening concern
facts.value: The patient reports sharp lower abdominal pain with light vaginal spotting.

provenance.quote: Sharp lower abdominal pain with light vaginal spotting.

facts.label: Pain onset
facts.value: Pain started suddenly this morning and is worse on the right side.

provenance.quote: Sudden right-sided lower abdominal pain this morning.

facts.label: Gynecologic history
facts.value: Her last menstrual period was about seven weeks ago.

provenance.quote: Last menstrual period approximately seven weeks ago.

facts.label: Risk history
facts.value: She has a history of pelvic inflammatory disease treated two years earlier.

provenance.quote: History of PID treated two years prior.

facts.label: Triage vitals
facts.value: Heart rate is 118/min, blood pressure 94/58 mmHg, respiratory rate 20/min, temperature 37.0 C.

facts.label: Pelvic and abdominal exam
facts.value: There is right adnexal tenderness, cervical motion tenderness, and mild lower abdominal guarding.

provenance.quote: Right adnexal tenderness, cervical motion tenderness, mild guarding.

facts.label: Transvaginal ultrasound
facts.value: Ultrasound shows no intrauterine gestational sac, a right adnexal mass, and a small amount of free pelvic fluid.

provenance.quote: No intrauterine gestation; right adnexal mass; small free fluid.

facts.label: Dangerous differential
facts.value: A pregnancy-related surgical emergency should remain high on the differential until excluded.

provenance.quote: Pelvic pain, bleeding, positive hCG, hypotension, and adnexal findings make this time-sensitive.

facts.label: Hint 1
facts.value: Do not separate the pregnancy test from the ultrasound result and vital signs.

provenance.quote: Positive hCG plus no intrauterine gestation and instability is the key pattern.

facts.label: Final diagnosis
facts.value: Ectopic pregnancy supported by pelvic pain, vaginal spotting, positive hCG, no intrauterine gestation, adnexal mass, and free fluid.
